findings.description: Overview page reads `overview.pending_review_count` and `overview.pending_claims_count` from the `/admin/pipeline/overview` API response, but the backend endpoint (`pipeline_overview` in backend/api/admin.py:54-73) only returns `job_counts` and `recent_submissions` — neither `pending_review_count` nor `pending_claims_count` exist in the response. Both values will always be `undefined`, falling back to `0` via `?? 0`. The Submissions and Claims stat cards will always show '0 pending'.
findings.resolution: Pass 2 verified: backend now queries pending counts and returns them in the response.

findings.description: Unnecessary deferred import inside function body: `import api.deps as _spend_history_deps` is used solely to access `settings.apify_cost_per_cu`. The `settings` object is already imported at module level (line 10: `from core.config import settings`) and is used directly in the existing spend endpoint (line 862: `settings.apify_cost_per_cu`). The deferred import adds indirection for no reason — use `settings.apify_cost_per_cu` directly, matching the pattern 10 lines above in the same file.
findings.resolution: Pass 2 verified: deferred import removed, settings.apify_cost_per_cu used directly.

findings.description: Broken indentation: the `return` statement and its arguments inside `formatUsd()` are at column 0 instead of being indented inside the function body. The code works because JavaScript doesn't care about indentation, but this is clearly a formatting error introduced by the diff. Lines 54-57 should be indented by 2 spaces to match the function's body indentation (lines 49-52).
findings.resolution: Pass 2 verified: SpendTab.tsx was not touched in the fix diff. Lines 54-57 remain unindented.

findings.description: Test mocks the internal `use-admin-auth` hook with `vi.mock()`, violating the project's 'mock at boundaries only' testing principle (CLAUDE.md). The hook is an internal module, not a system boundary. Other test files in this PR (e.g., SpendHistoryChart.test.tsx) correctly pass `getToken` as a prop instead of mocking the hook. This test should render SubmissionsPage with a mocked fetch (boundary) and let the real hook execute, or refactor to test the SubmissionsTab component directly.
findings.resolution: Pass 2 verified: vi.mock('../_hooks/use-admin-auth') still present at line 6. Not addressed in fix diff.

findings.description: Test only asserts `expect(document.body).toBeTruthy()` which is always true regardless of whether the component rendered correctly. This is a no-op assertion — it provides zero regression protection. Should assert on actual rendered content (e.g., the 'Submissions' heading).
findings.resolution: Pass 2 verified: assertion changed to `screen.getByText('Loading...')` which validates actual rendered content.

findings.description: `beforeEach` is used outside a `describe` block without being imported from vitest. It relies on the global `beforeEach` which may not be configured in all vitest setups. The import at line 2 includes `describe, it, expect, vi` but not `beforeEach`.
findings.resolution: Pass 2 verified: `beforeEach` added to vitest import on line 2.

re_verify_notes: Test Philosophy Minor finding (mock at boundaries) remains. Standards & Conventions Important finding (SpendTab.tsx indentation) remains. No new issues introduced by fixes.
